:::{#thm-collapsibility}

#### Which estimands are collapsible?

For **causal** estimands (@def-causal-marginal-risk),
and under the identification assumptions of @thm-causal-marginal-risk,
the collapsibility criterion (@def-collapsibility) is evaluated with respect to the **marginal** distribution $f_Z(z)$.
Additive (difference) measures are collapsible;
ratio and log-odds measures are not collapsible in general.

For **observational** estimands (@def-observed-marginal-risk),
the marginal measure uses the conditional distribution
$f_{Z \mid A}(z \mid a)$ as weights rather than the marginal $f_Z(z)$.
Collapsibility with respect to $f_Z$ therefore holds
in particular when $Z \perp A$ (no confounding):
then $f_{Z \mid A}(z \mid a) = f_Z(z)$,
so $\pi(a) = \E{\pi(a,Z) \mid A = a} \overset{Z \perp A}{=} \E{\pi(a,Z)}$
(@thm-observed-marginal-risk) —
the observational marginal risk equals its $f_Z$-weighted stratum average,
which is collapsibility with respect to $f_Z$ and needs only $Z \perp A$,
not the full identification assumptions.

::: {.proof}

Additive measures are collapsible by linearity of expectation,
applying the causal-marginal-risk identity (@thm-causal-marginal-risk)
to each potential probability:

$$
\ba
\pi_1 - \pi_0
&= \E{\pi(1,Z)} - \E{\pi(0,Z)}
&& \text{[causal marginal risk]}
\\
&= \E{\pi(1,Z) - \pi(0,Z)}.
&& \text{[linearity of expectation]}
\ea
$$

Ratio and log-odds measures are not collapsible in general:
@exm-collapsibility gives a counterexample
in which the marginal risk ratio and odds ratio
differ from the marginal-distribution-weighted average
of their stratum-specific values.

:::

:::
